element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Pickup game or season mode opening matches

element_introduction_order.element: Field hazards (landmines, fire pits, open space)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Integrated throughout all matches; spatial awareness required
Cascading effects: casualties lead to forfeits

element_introduction_order.element: Nasty Audibles (dirty tricks: explosives, invisibility, jets, shocks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic deployment for field advantage; risk/reward decision-making
Once per half bribery limit; risk of ref death triggering tougher replacement

element_introduction_order.element: Varied field surfaces (rock, ice, toxic waste, rubber)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Affects player movement and hazard interaction dynamics

element_introduction_order.element: Championship finale (MVP induction into Hole of Flame)

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a match without team elimination
typical_tension_triggers: Proximity to field hazards during active play
QB in danger (opposing team attempting QB bash)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turnover-driven, match-based pacing with escalating chaos; tension builds through accumulated player losses and hazard encounters; relief comes from successful plays and match conclusions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to core mechanics and hazard avoidance; foundational strategy with manageable casualties
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing reliance on Nasty Audibles; field hazards become more integral to tactical play; opponent aggression escalates
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship matches demand sophisticated audible timing and team composition strategy; high-consequence decisions; team elimination as forfeit condition
notable_spikes: First introduction of multiple hazard types in single field
Opponent team's strategic use of Nasty Audibles

notable_level_patterns.description: Each match occurs on a field with unique hazard placement (landmines, fire pits, open space gaps). Field surface type (rock, ice, toxic waste, rubber) modifies player movement and hazard interaction, creating distinct tactical environments.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Season mode matches vs. different teams

notable_level_patterns.description: Matches progress from standard play into hazard-heavy scenarios; player eliminations trigger forfeits if threshold is crossed; successive plays compound field danger as teams position for dominance.

Nasty Audibles serve as discrete tension spikes; each audible (exploding ball, invisibility, electric shock, jet pack, QB assassination) creates unpredictable moment of high consequence, followed by play resolution relief.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game matches with strategic audible usage

notable_level_patterns.description: Bribery mechanic (once per half) creates tension over whether to use resource and accept risk of referee assassination, which triggers replacement with tougher referee and penalty escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Matches where bribery timing is critical to score management
